shows.title: Space Updates / Compromised Childhoods
shows.first_half: First Half: Author, journalist, and historian Rod Pyle will bring us the latest on the new space race with China, the Artemis lunar landing program, as well as Elon Musk's SpaceX. He'll also examine Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin lunar landers and discuss what's new in the robotic exploration of the solar system.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Master herbalist and classical homeopath Sara Chana will discuss the overmedicating of our children at the expense of their ability to grow, think, and thrive. She'll explore how today's high-pressure, screen-saturated world is reshaping the childhood of many kids.

shows.title: Ancient Legends & Symbols / The Myth of Free Will
shows.first_half: First Half: Author Hans Dietrich will explore ancient civilizations, legends and lore, the magic of symbols, and how these symbols include references to possible extraterrestrial visitations preserved within ancient Buddhist texts. He'll also present his research examining Khemetian cosmology's solar cycles and the concept of sacred houses.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Psychic and medium Vincent Genna will discuss the uncomfortable truth that most of what you think has been installed and conditioned by forces you never chose. He'll explain why free will is far more limited than people believe.

shows.title: UFO Retrieval Cases / Aliens in Antiquity
shows.first_half: First Half: Private pilot and military aerospace historian Michael Schratt has lectured across the country on ''mystery aircraft'' and classified propulsion systems buried deep within the military industrial complex. He'll explore some of the top UFO crash and retrieval cases as compiled by Leonard Stringfield.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Reverend Michael J. Carter will discuss the existence of extraterrestrials in antiquity and how their presence shaped our sacred scriptures. He'll also explain how those ancient visits influence biblical prophecy today.

shows.title: Music of the 60s & 70s / Famous Urban Legends
shows.first_half: First Half: Author and child of Hollywood Harvey Kubernik will discuss the enduring popularity of music and culture from the 1960s and 70s. He'll talk about the pop and rock acts still performing in legendary venues, as well as the evolution of audio and visual media into the streaming era.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Author Belle Zimet will share paranormal urban legends, such as Bloody Mary, the vanishing hitchhiker, tommyknockers, the dark watchers, Hawaiian night marchers, and ghost ships. She'll explain why stories like these fascinate us, their murky origins, and their impact on society.

shows.title: Classic Paranormal Films / Understanding Dark Forces
shows.first_half: First Half: Filmmaker, poet, and professor Gary D. Rhodes, PhD will examine pseudo-scientific documentaries made from approximately 1970 to 1982, including film topics ranging from ancient aliens to monsterous cryptids. He'll also talk about the birth of horror movies, Bela Lugosi, Dracula, and more.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Author Kaedrich Olsen will discuss spirit possession, witches, and the presence of so-called evil forces. He'll explain what people are truly encountering, detailing what's actually happening beneath the surface that most don't understand.

shows.title: Greatest of All Treasures / Open Lines
shows.first_half: Guest host Connie Willis (info) speaks with treasure hunter Martin Bayerle about what he regards as the greatest treasure never recovered. He'll talk about the place where the largest concentrated gold cargo in history may be held, and why it has remained untouched for over a century. Followed by Open Lines in the latter half—give Connie a call and discuss whatever's on your mind!
